mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5% indicates tightening labor markets, which drives corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 4.5% fall below 0.5% on average, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of GDPC1 and HOUST on SPX returns becomes non-significant (p > 0.10), or their interaction term's coefficient drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side constraints, which raises inflation expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO decline periods exceed 1.0% on average, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.10 in rolling window tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in CPI > 2.5% periods remain positive, or the correlation between CPI and SPX returns falls below -0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weakening labor demand, which signals economic slowdown and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in PAYEMS decline periods exceed 0.8% on average, or the predictive accuracy of PAYEMS drops below 0.35 in rolling window tests.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates weak demand fundamentals, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST loses significance (p > 0.15), or SPX returns in divergent periods exceed -0.5% on average.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising oil prices (not in catalog) would signal supply shocks, but since oil prices are unavailable, this hypothesis uses INDPRO alone to predict negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in INDPRO decline periods exceed 1.5% on average, or the predictive power of INDPRO drops below 0.12 in out-of-sample tests.
